text: During executing your first project, your customer has terminated your project before it is complete. Which of the following statements is true?
explanation: Even if a project is terminated before the work is completed, you still need to document the lessons learned and update the OPA. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: You must stop all work and release the team immediately.
You must work with the team to document the lessons learned.
You must keep the team working on the project to give your senior management time to talk to the customer.
You should give your team a vacation till you discuss with the customer.

text: Patrick is a project manager for a construction project. He is using root cause analysis to figure out what could cause potential risks on his project. Which process is he involved in?
explanation: In Identify Risks process you must find root cause of risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 415

choices.text: Identify Risks
Plan Quality Management
Manage Quality
Plan Risk Responses

text: Amit is the project manager of a software project to create a mobile application that is scheduled to be completed in four months. The project charter is signed and Amit is working now to collect requirements from project stakeholders. The customer requested that the design of the project should be completed after one month. In which document should this condition be documented?
explanation: This condition is a milestone date and it is considered as a constraint. Constraints are documented in the assumption log. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 155 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 83

choices.text: Requirements traceability matrix
Assumptions log
Scope management plan
Requirements management plan

text: You are managing a large construction project. Part of the project work will be outsourced to a subcontractor. A team member asks about your role as a project manager regarding any procurement in your project. What should you tell him?
explanation: The main goal for any procurement in a project is to transfer risks from buyer to seller. So the project manager must provide an understanding for project risks to ensure that contract terms and conditions transfer all risks from buyer to seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 463 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 74–76

choices.text: The project manager should tell the procurement manager how the procurement process should be handled.
The project manager should negotiate with the prospective sellers.
The project manager should provide an understanding of the risks of the project.
The project manager should evaluate seller proposals and select the best one.

text: You are a project manager for a tele-communication project, the project is to swap the old network for 2 cities with a new network. You have been asked by top management to cut the project costs by 10% after you submitted them. The top management expect 25% improvement in drop calls as a result of the project. There are 35 stakeholders in the project and the scope of the project is unclear. What is your best course of action?
explanation: If your estimates are accurate then you are ethically and professionally responsible for supporting them. If there is a need to cut project costs then the top management should help the project manager in finding another solution and to evaluate its impacts on project scope, schedule,..etc Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 241 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 55

choices.text: Re-plan to achieve a 35% improvement in drop calls.
Provide an accurate estimate of the actual costs and be able to support your estimates.
Reduce the estimate by 10% as required and note the change in the risk management plan.
Meet with the team to identify where you can find 10% savings.

text: You are a project manager on a construction project. When you planned the project, your enterprise environmental factors included a policy that all changes that cost over 2% of the budget need to be approved by the CEO, but smaller changes could be paid for by a management contingency fund. One of your stakeholders submitted a change request that requires a 3% increase in the budget. Your company has an outsourcing effort, and you believe that a small change to the way that the change is requested could allow you to take advantage of it and cut your costs in half. What is the best way to handle this situation?
explanation: As the company you work for has policies and procedures, you must follow them. Here the change requires a 3% increase in budget, so as per the company policy, you should request approval from the company CEO. When a stakeholder submitted the change request, this means that the change request is documented. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 83 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Work with the stakeholder to figure out how to reduce the cost of the change.
Request approval from the CEO.
Refuse the change because it is over 2% of the budget.
Document the change request, since all changes must be documented.

text: A project manager facilitates a discussion with the team about experimenting with some agile practices in their plan-driven project. The team has been working on traditional projects for years and have never used any agile practices before. The project manager suggests using product reviews. One of the team members asks him about iteration reviews "is it similar to any traditional process?". Which process should the project manager mention in his answer?
explanation: Product review is similar to the Validate Scope process as the team obtains a stakeholder's acceptance for a project deliverable. However product reviews occur more frequently as it is done for each iteration in the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 163 and Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Validate Scope
Control Quality
Close Project or Phase
No, it is not similar to any traditional process.

text: Mark is a project manager in a construction company. He is managing a construction project to build a hospital in a new city. He was informed that a new water line is being installed in the neighborhood beside the hospital under construction. Organizational policy requires that some forms for city environmental changes need to be filled out before his team can continue work on the project. This is an example of:
explanation: As the project manager fills a form required by organizational policy, so this is a good example for an enterprise environmental factor. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 38

choices.text: An organizational process asset
A program
An enterprise environmental factor
A project

text: Martha is a scrum master for a product development project. Midway during a sprint, the customer representative has approached her to add a new, complex, and urgent safety-related requirement to the current sprint. The new requirement can't be postponed to another sprint. What is Martha's best response?
explanation: Normally, changes should not happen during a sprint for the current items in the sprint backlog. However, under unusual conditions, scope may be re-negotiated between the product owner and the team even if the sprint may be cancelled. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55.

choices.text: Inform the customer representative that the sprint has already started and we can't add new requirements to the current sprint.
Add the new requirement to the product backlog, so that the product owner can give it the highest priority to be added to the next sprint.
Add the new requirement to the sprint backlog and if needed, the team can move a low-priority requirement back to the product backlog.
Discuss the new requirement with the product owner, who may cancel the current sprint.

text: A project is being implemented in an organization to prepare a chemical compound and it is required to follow strict regulatory compliance guidelines. The project manager wants to ensure adherence to these guidelines as they are mandatory requirements for the project. What should the project manager do?
explanation: Adherence to regulatory compliance is a requirement, it must be managed as part of the project scope and not as best effort. The best answer is to assign this requirement to team member. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 27

choices.text: Assign a team member as responsible to ensure regulatory compliance and update RACI matrix accordingly.
Check regulatory compliance adherence during project closure.
Submit a change request to allocate enough time, cost and resources to accommodate regulatory compliance.
Manage adherence to regulatory compliance as best effort according to available time, budget and resources.

text: All of the following are examples of motivation models that can be used by the project manager to understanding what motivates project team members and other stakeholders, except:
explanation: Motivation models include theory X, theory Y, and theory Z, hygiene factors, theory of needs and intrinsic and extrinsic motivation. Drexler/Sibbet is a team performance model. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 167

choices.text: Theory X, Theory Y, and Theory Z
Drexler/Sibbet
Hygiene factors
Theory of needs

text: You are managing a software project. One of your team members informed you that he has added an extra functionality in the work package assigned to him which will help make the usage of the product easier for the customer. The customer didn't require to add this functionality, but it seems that it will make the usage of the product easier. The team member added this functionality to exceed customer expectation. What should you do first in this situation?
explanation: You must first understand the added functionality before taking any decision. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 87

choices.text: Understand what is the added functionality.
Instruct the team member to remove the added functionality.
Submit a change request to add the new functionality to the scope baseline and calculate the time and cost needed for it.
Reprimand the team member for adding extra functionality.

text: What is the focus of the Control Resources process?
explanation: Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and taking corrective action as necessary. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 352

choices.text: Ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned.
Ensuring that the physical and human resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned.
Ensuring that the physical, human and monetary resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned.
Ensuring that the human resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned.

text: You are the CEO of a cars maintenance center and desire to improve project management practices in your company. What is the best thing to do in order to manage the maintenance of each car?
explanation: The project is a temporary endeavor, it has a beginning and an end and it produces unique deliverables. In this situation, the cars maintenance centers doesn't perform projects but it performs operational work (maintenance is ongoing and repetitive work). So functional organizations are more suitable for operational work. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 16

choices.text: Identify all the stakeholders for this project.
Issue a project charter for each car.
Retain the functional organizational structure.
Set up a PMO.

text: You are managing a four-phase construction project. The customer has formally accepted the deliverable of the first phase and the first phase gate has been reached. You and the key stakeholders want to make sure that the phase has met its success criteria and whether you can proceed to the next phase or not. What are the required documents in this situation?
explanation: When closing the project or phase , the project manager reviews the project management plan to ensure that all project work is completed and that the project has met its objectives. A phase gate is also a review point at the end of a phase to determine if the project will continue to the next phase, end the project, repeat the phase, or continue with some modifications. The documents required to make this determination are the project charter, the project management plan, the business case, and the benefits management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Lessons learned register
Risk register and risk report
Contracts, procurement documents and project status report
Project charter, business documents and project management plan.

text: All of the following are against Agile Principle Number Two "Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage", except:
explanation: As expressed in Agile Manifesto Principle 2, staying flexible to meet the customer’s changing business needs is fundamental to agile, and the backlog is one of the key ways that agile teams ensure this adaptability. The backlog is always evolving, and it needs to be kept continually updated with the latest requirements and new information. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8.

choices.text: Change Control Board
Scope creep
Scope variance
Backlog refinement

text: All of the following statements about Control Resources process is true, except:
explanation: The resources needed for the project should be assigned and released at the right time not as early as possible, right place, and right amount for the project to continue without delays. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 353

choices.text: Control Resources process should be performed continuously in all project phases and throughout the project life cycle.
The resources needed for the project should be assigned and released as early as possible, at the right place, and right amount for the project to continue without delays.
The Control Resources process is concerned with physical resources only.
The Control Resources process is not concerned with project human resources.

text: A project manager is executing a construction project. He is working with the quality assurance team to ensure that the project will satisfy the quality standards and metrics. Which of the following must they have before they start this process?
explanation: Quality control measurements are used to analyze and evaluate the quality of the processes and deliverables of the project against the standards of the performing organization or the requirements specified. Quality control measurements can also compare the processes used to create the measurements and validate actual measurements to determine their level of correctness. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 288

choices.text: Quality control measurements
Quality assurance plan
Verified deliverables
Quality reports

text: Which of the following is the most beneficial use of the critical path?
explanation: The critical path is the sequence of activities that represents the longest path through a project, so any delay in the activities on the critical path will delay the project. So the activities on the critical path need immediate attention. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: To know where you have a spike in the use of resources.
To know where there is float on the project.
To know where to assign extra resources.
To know which activities need immediate attention.

text: Which of the following is the purpose of project status meetings?
explanation: The project status meeting is the best method to discuss the status of the project and exchange information about the project. Other choices can be done outside project status meeting. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 376 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 180

choices.text: Exchange information about the project.
Approving project changes.
Have team members report on what they are doing.
Issue project status reports.

text: In which procurement management process is the source selection criteria used in your project?
explanation: Source selection criteria is created during the Plan Procurement Management process and used to select the seller in the Conduct Procurement process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 486

choices.text: Conduct Procurements
Control Procurements
Close Project or Phase
Plan Procurement Management

text: Project team members are estimating the costs of the activities that will be performed during the project. They are using three point estimating. The expected cost of the activity based on weighted average distribution equals 500$. The cost of the activity, based on realistic effort assessment for the required work and any predicted expenses equals 480$. The cost based on analysis of the best-case scenario for the activity equals 420$. What is The cost based on analysis of the worst-case scenario for the activity?
explanation: Beta distribution. cE = (cO + 4cM + cP) / 6. cM = 480, cO = 420 , cE = 500. cP = 6cE - cO - 4cM = 6*500 - 420 - 4*480 = 660$ Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 245*

text: The main difference between stakeholders and key stakeholders is that the key stakeholders are:
explanation: All other choices are valid for stakeholders, while key stakeholders are decision makers. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 12

choices.text: A person, group, or organization that is affected by the project.
A person, group, or organization that is in a decision making position.
A person, group, or organization that affects the project.
A person, group, or organization that executes the project work.

text: Smith has just been assigned as the manager of a project to build an electric sub-station that is part of a major electric power distribution system in a new city. The company management decided to run the project two weeks before his assignment and without his involvement. Some basic decisions on deliverables, staffing, budgeting and completion date have already been made as well. Which of the following best describes what he should do first?
explanation: This is a new project which must have a project charter to formally authorize the existence of the project, provide the project manager with authority and link the project to the strategy and ongoing work of the organization. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 75 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 140

choices.text: Create a project charter which links the project to the strategy and ongoing work of the organization and documents the high level plan.
Create a project scope statement for his project that describes the detailed project scope and product scope.
Start identifying stakeholders for his project and collect their requirements and expectations.
Begin working on the project management plan.

text: A project team is working on a software project using an adaptive approach. Midway through the project, a key stakeholder asked the project team about the features which have been completed over time, features which are still in development, and those that are still in the backlog. To which of the following artifacts can the project team guide him?
explanation: Cumulative flow diagram (CFD) indicates features completed over time, features in development, and those in the backlog. It may also include features at intermediate states, such as features designed but not yet constructed, those in quality assurance, or those in testing. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 188

choices.text: Roadmap
Burndown chart
Burnup chart
Cumulative flow diagram (CFD)

text: Richard is managing an online shopping website development project. He and the project team have finished the website design and got it approved from the customer and the project board. Later, they have noticed that there is a technical bug in the website design which may impact the website performance in the future. To fix this bug, a new design is needed which might delay the project by one month. What should Richard do?
explanation: As a part of the professional and social responsibility of the project manager, he can’t continue working on a defective design. The project manager should first identify a risk of a design delay, then he can raise a change request with the new design. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: As the design has been approved, he should continue the project execution with the approved design.
Add a design delay risk in the project risk register.
Raise a change request for the new design and assess the impacts.
Escalate this topic to the project sponsor and let him take the decision.

text: Jerry is managing a software project. He calls for a meeting with the project team to discuss the current variances. He receives a weekly report from the project team which includes earned value measurements for the project at this point. Which of the following variances contributes to the least risk in the project?
explanation: BAC is how much did we BUDGET for the TOTAL project effort. EAC is what do we currently expect the TOTAL project to cost. If BAC is greater than EAC this means that the planned budget is greater than the estimated budget, so there is enough funds for the project, which means risk is less. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

choices.text: EAC is greater than BAC
BAC is greater than EAC
TCPI = 1.08
ETC is greater than BAC

text: Tom is the sponsor of a large construction project. He is meeting with all the stakeholders to ask for their participation and support in the next testing events. How would you best describe his level of engagement?
explanation: Here Tom is trying to increase stakeholders engagement, so he has a leading role. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 521

choices.text: Unaware
Resistant
Supportive
Leading

text: The project sponsor has asked the project team to increase the frequency of sending the project status report instead of weekly to be daily. Moreover, he has asked also to send a daily report for the progress of outstanding tasks. The team objects to the sponsor's request because preparing such report takes much time. Which of the following is the best course of action for the project manager?
explanation: The project manager should review the communication management plan which describes the type, frequency and responsible person for sending each report. If it needs to be changed, it must be done through a change request as it is a part of the project management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 79 and 186

choices.text: Ask the sponsor to hire additional resources.
Try to convince both sides to accept sending the report twice a week.
Review the communication management plan and submit a change request if needed.
Meet with the project sponsor and convince him that this is too much work for the team members to do.

text: During a major construction project in a predictive environment, a team member came to the project manager with a change request. The change has a very good idea and will be useful to the project. The project manager asked him to write a report describing the benefits of the change. What is the most appropriate advice for this situation?
explanation: The cost-benefit analysis done during project planning is not related to the change. The project sponsor doesn't approve change requests. What is meant by cost-benefit analysis here is the assumptions which the team member should provide when he requires the change. So the team member should do what was asked so that the project manager can make a decision based on the information provided. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: He should remind the project manager that the cost-benefit analysis for the project was done during project planning.
The project manager should do this report himself as a part of his job.
The team member should do what was asked because this sort of information must be given to the project sponsor to make the change.
A project manager must be able to weigh the benefits of the change versus the costs and compare them to other possible changes. The team member should do what was asked.

text: ABC is a large organization which has a long history of managing projects in predictive environment. Recently, there is an initiative for switching to hybrid environment as there is a high demand on projects that require agile and hybrid development approaches. Which of the following transition strategies is the most appropriate?
explanation: A gradual transition involves adding more iterative techniques to improve learning and alignment among teams and stakeholders. Later, consider adding more incremental techniques to accelerate value and return on investment to sponsors. Try these new techniques on a less risky project with a medium- to low-degree of uncertainty. Then, when the organization is successful with a hybrid approach, try more complex projects that require more of those techniques to be added. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 30

choices.text: Fixing a due date for transition after a reasonable period and ask teams to learn new approach before deadline.
An abrupt transition by switching to hybrid immediately will force the teams to follow and learn new approach.
A gradual transition by adding more iterative techniques to improve learning and alignment among teams and stakeholders.
Hiring new employees with experience in agile and hybrid projects.

text: Thomas is a project manager for a large software project. He and his team are creating the project schedule. The project sponsor requested from Thomas to create a chart to use in reporting the project schedule to him. This chart should identify the scheduled start or completion of major deliverables and key external interfaces. What should he create?
explanation: Milestone charts are similar to bar charts, but only identify the scheduled start or completion of major deliverables and key external interfaces. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 218

choices.text: Work breakdown structure
Project schedule network diagram
Gantt chart
Milestone chart

text: A prompt list is a predetermined list of:
explanation: A prompt list is a predetermined list of risk categories that might give rise to individual project risks and that could also act as sources of overall project risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 416.

choices.text: Risks
Threats
Risk categories
Opportunities

text: You are working on a software project using adaptive approach when a key stakeholder has informs you that he needs to give feature E a higher priority than other features and implement it in the next iteration. What should you do?
explanation: The product owner is responsible for guiding the direction of the product. Product owners rank the work based on its business value. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 52

choices.text: Respond politely, inform the stakeholder that his request can't be implemented because customer has given other features higher priority.
Inform the project sponsor about this request and ask him to take the appropriate decision.
Respond politely and inform the stakeholder that his request can't be implemented because some other features have higher business values.
Inform the stakeholder that you will discuss his request with the product owner.

text: Which of the following statements best describes a risk owner?
explanation: A risk owner is a person who takes responsibility for each agreed-to and funded risk response. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 426

choices.text: The person who is responsible for the response plan for the risk.
The person who monitors the watchlist that contains the risk.
The person who identifies the risk.
The person who evaluates the probability and impact of a risk.

text: You are managing a complex construction project that resulted in a schedule that is too long and has too many activities. Alex is a scheduling professional expert in your organization, he is auditing your project schedule network diagram. The expert tells you to focus more on nodes with "path convergence". What does he mean?
explanation: When there are multiple predecessor activities, dependency increases and risk increases. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 190 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 60

choices.text: Nodes in a WBS, where several work packages are brought together.
Nodes in a network diagram which have multiple successors.
Nodes in a decision tree, showing various branches with the same chance.
Nodes in a network diagram which have multiple predecessors.

text: A project has been initiated in an organization to produce a new electric device. Midway through the project, a global shortage for one of the electric components used in the device has occurred. The project team has investigated many approaches to resolve this issue and came up with three possible options. They want to select the best option. This situation represents:
explanation: Situational ambiguity surfaces when more than one outcome is possible. Having multiple options to solve a problem is a form of situational ambiguity. Solutions for exploration of ambiguity include progressive elaboration, experimentation, and the use of prototypes. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 117

choices.text: Volatility
Uncertainty
Conceptual Ambiguity
Situational Ambiguity

text: You are managing a construction project. Two team members come to you with a conflict. One of them has identified an important project risk: that the cement supplier may not deliver the needed quantity of cement on time. The other team member doesn’t believe that the risk is likely to happen; however, you consult the lessons learned from previous projects and discover that the supplier failed to deliver the needed quantity of cement on three previous projects. You decide that the risk is a major one; you terminate the contract with the supplier, and instead, you agree with other suppliers to deliver the needed quantity. Both team members agree that this has eliminated the risk. Which of the following best describes your risk response strategy?
explanation: Avoidance means removing the risk by removing the cause, and here you removed the supplier who causes the risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123.

choices.text: Transference
Mitigation
Avoidance
Acceptance

text: You have been assigned as a project manager for a new construction project. The sponsor has issued the charter and you have compiled a list of potential stakeholders. You are classifying them with regard to the direction of their influence. The project sponsor is classified as:
explanation: Senior management of the performing organization or customer organization, sponsor, and steering committee are classified as upward stakeholders. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 513

choices.text: Upward
Downward
Outward
Sideward

text: Mary has been assigned to manage a new project. In order to measure the project complexity, she decides to use Stacey matrix. Which two dimensions will she use to determine the relative complexity of her project?
explanation: Stacey matrix looks at two dimensions to determine the relative complexity of a project: (a) the relative uncertainty of the requirements for the deliverable, and (b) the relative uncertainty of the technology that will be used to create the deliverable. Based on the relative uncertainty of these dimensions, a project is considered simple, complicated, complex, or chaotic. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 165

choices.text: The relative uncertainty of the requirements for the deliverable, and the relative uncertainty of the technology that will be used to create the deliverable.
The relative accuracy of the project estimates and the rate of changing initial requirements.
The project expected return on investment ROI and the total contract value.
The project net present value and the project budget.

text: The project manager for the new project notices that the meeting room seats and air conditioner are not good. She turns her attention to influencing the organization to improve the team’s working conditions. Which motivational theory is she following?
explanation: Herzber theory deals with motivating agents and hygiene factors like working conditions, salary and work relationships. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 158

choices.text: Maslow’s hierarchy
Expectancy theory
Herzberg
McGregor

text: Peter is a project manager for a tele-communication project in a multinational company. The project team members are located in three countries with different time zones. As per the communications management plan, the project team has a weekly status meeting through videoconferencing. Which communication method are they using?
explanation: Interactive communication is between two or more parties performing a multidirectional exchange of information in real time. It employs communications artifacts such as meetings, phone calls, instant messaging, some forms of social media, and videoconferencing. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: Push communication
Pull communication
Formal communication
Interactive communication

text: Brian has just joined a large construction company as a project manager. He has been assigned to manage his first project in the company. Midway through the project, he noticed that the customer is getting frequent change requests implemented by informally approaching the team members. This has resulted in work disruption and discontent among team members. What should be his first priority to remedy the situation?
explanation: Controlling the project scope ensures all requested changes and recommended corrective or preventive actions are processed through the Perform Integrated Change Control process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 168 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 87.

choices.text: Begin creating a detailed project scope statement.
Call for a meeting with the customer and team members to solve the problem.
Modify the project management plan so that all change requests must be approved by you.
Ensure proper project scope control.

text: You are an agile practitioner working on an agile project to create a customer facing website for an electronic devices shop. The team has developed a working prototype that you and the product owner are delighted with. However, when you show it to the sponsor, the sponsor complains that the website doesn't look as professional as the other competitors' websites. How should you respond?
explanation: A minimum viable product MVP is a package of functionality that is complete enough to be useful to the users or the market, yet still small enough that it does not represent the entire project. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 23

choices.text: This sample just tests the product concept, features, and technology, it's not the final release version.
This is just the minimum viable product; it will be improved in the next release.
The product owner has confirmed that this prototype includes all the features that are required.
If you want a more polished website we will need to add a design specialist to the team.

text: An organization wants to select one of two projects to implement. Project A is expected to result in a $30 million net profit, while project B is expected to result in $25 net profit. The organization does not have the capability to undertake both projects. If the organization selects project A, what will be the opportunity cost?
explanation: The opportunity cost is the cost of the project which has not been selected. Domain = Business Environment.

choices.text: $5 million
$25 million
$30 million
$55 million

text: Which of the following statements is an example of enhancing an opportunity?
explanation: Enhance means increase probability or impact. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 444 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: By forming a partnership with another company, the project’s value will increase for both companies.
By taking additional actions, you increase the probability of potential profit.
By taking out insurance, you can reduce potential costs to the project.
By documenting the opportunity in the watchlist, you can keep track of it and ensure it gets exploited.

text: Luis is a project manager in an electronics firm. He has been assigned to manage a new project to develop a faster, cheaper, and smaller laptop. He is analyzing the defects found in the product by graphical representation of this numerical data. The graph shows the ranking of the cause of defects and the number of defects found per deliverable. Which data representation technique is he using?
explanation: Histograms show a graphical representation of numerical data. Histograms can show the number of defects per deliverable, a ranking of the cause of defects, the number of times each process is noncompliant, or other representations of project or product defects. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 293

choices.text: Histogram
Scatter Diagram
Matrix diagram
Cause-and-effect diagram

text: A project manager for a new electronic chips manufacturing project needs to make sure that the deliverables meet the quality standards. The project team produces about 100,000 chips per day, so testing all the deliverables would take much time and cost. The chips are so small that inspection would destroy them. Under these circumstances, what should the project manager do?
explanation: You need to take a valid sample of the products of the project if inspecting the entire population would take much time, or if inspecting the products would be destructive. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 303

choices.text: Inspect a sample of the deliverables.
Outsource the inspection to another company with more expertise.
Test all the deliverables to meet the quality standard.
Form a quality assurance team to audit the manufacturing process.

text: Mike is a procurement specialist who has worked before for a construction firm performing plan-driven projects. He has recently joined ABC organization which has a long history of performing change-driven projects. He asks an agile practitioner about the main differences between change-driven and plan-driven contracts. How should the agile practitioner answer his question?
explanation: On agile projects, procurement is challenging since scope details can't be defined in details prior to contract writing. Also the intangible nature of knowledge work products can make it difficult to evaluate and get acceptance. So agile contracts should be designed to accommodate changes as scope may need to be re-prioritized during the project for business purpose. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 77

choices.text: Change-driven contracts should allow scope early completion for business purpose.
Change-driven contracts should be time and material as scope may change, while plan-driven contracts should be fixed price as scope is fixed.
Change-driven contracts should allow scope reprioritization for business purpose.
Agile values customer collaboration over contract negotiation.

text: You are a project manager for a data center upgrade project. The data center uses old systems and your project was to upgrade to a new system. The project was planned to be in winter were heavy rains are likely to happen. The heavy rains caused power outages just when the project was near completion. When the power was restored, all of the project customer’s data were lost with no way of retrieving them. What should have been done to prevent this problem?
explanation: The risk here is data loss due to power outage. So insurance against this risk will not solve the problem (will not restore lost data). Planning for a reserve fund is a risk acceptance and it will not restore lost data. Scheduling the installation during summer is a risk avoidance and may solve the problem, but it may have a negative impact on project schedule, so it is not the best solution. The best solution is to monitor the weather and have a contingency plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Schedule the installation in summer.
Plan for a reserve fund.
Insure against this risk.
Monitor the weather and have a contingency plan.

text: The project team working on a construction project noticed that stakeholders are not engaged enough and they only share negative feedback about the project. However, the project team usually communicates with stakeholders. All of the following might be reasons behind this issue, except:
explanation: Evaluate whether sufficient information is being shared with stakeholders; feedback loops are present and working; and deeper engagement may work better than simply communicating. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: Insufficient information is being shared with stakeholders.
All stakeholders contributed in creating the project management plan.
Feedback loops are not present or not working.
The project team don't engage deeper with stakeholders.

text: Julian is managing a construction project. He has determined that an activity is too risky to be completed internally. So, he hires a contractor to complete the activity. However, the contractor may not complete the work within budget, which may cause an increase in the project budget. What is the type of this risk?
explanation: Secondary risks are risks that arise as a direct result of implementing a risk response. In this situation, the response was work transference to a contractor, which resulted in a secondary risk: an increase in the project budget. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 439 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125.

choices.text: Residual risk
Secondary risk
Transference
Accepted risk

text: The project team members in a construction project are suffering from some bottlenecks presented in long times waiting for approvals on invoices, change requests, and signing some documents. According to the existing processes, only very few persons are authorized to do this. How can this issue be best tackled?
explanation: The approval process needs to be tailored by streamlining approval decisions through fewer people authorized to make decisions up to certain value thresholds. For example approval within certain amount to be approved by X and above this amount to be approved by Y and so on. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: Try to reduce the items that need approval as much as possible.
Team members should keep reminding the approvers till their dependencies are cleared out.
Account for the delays due to bottlenecks in the contingency reserve.
Tailor the existing processes by streamlining approval decisions through fewer people authorized to make decisions up to certain value thresholds.

text: Thomas is the finance manager in the organization and he is known to be an unsupportive stakeholder. He is a key stakeholder in the new IT project. Harold has been assigned as a project manager for this project. He held a meeting with Thomas to identify the preferred communication method, format, and content for planned communication activities. Which skill did Harold use?
explanation: Communication styles assessment is a technique used to assess communication styles and identify the preferred communication method, format, and content for planned communication activities. Often used with unsupportive stakeholders. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 375

choices.text: Communication styles assessment
Political awareness
Cultural awareness
Problem solving

text: What is the purpose of the communications management plan?
explanation: The communications management plan is a component of the project management plan that describes how project communications will be planned, structured, implemented, and monitored for effectiveness. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 79 and 186

choices.text: The project management plan is a subsidiary plan of the overall communications management plan.
The communications management plan describes why you need to communicate with each stakeholders.
The communications management plan describes the information delivery needs including format and level of detail.
The communications management plan focuses on the use of communications technologies by the project management team.

text: Mary, the project manager, sent a text message to Tom, a project stakeholder, using the company's instant messaging system. When he received it, Tom called her back. Which of the following is true about this situation?
explanation: Encoding mean that thoughts or ideas are translated (encoded) into language by the sender. Decoding means that the message is translated by the receiver back into meaningful thoughts or ideas. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

choices.text: Mary encoded the text message; Tom decoded it, and then encoded his feedback message.
Mary decoded her text message; Tom encoded his phone call and decoded the feedback.
Tom sent feedback to Mary, who encoded it.
Mary decoded her text message and Tom encoded his feedback.

text: Sam is leading a large project within the organization to switch the manual way of handling customer requests to an automated way. The project is associated with huge changes to organizations systems, employees behaviors and the organization culture as a whole, so it is expected to face a huge resistance from many stakeholders. Which of the following models can he use to transition from the current to the future desired state?
explanation: Many projects contain an aspect of changing systems, behaviors, activities, and sometimes, cultures. Managing this type of change requires thinking about how to transition from the current to the future desired state. There are many models that describe the activities necessary for successful change management like ADKAR model which focuses on five sequential steps that individuals undergo when adapting to change awareness, desire, knowledge, ability and reinforcement.

choices.text: ADKAR
OSCAR
McGregor
Cynefin Framework

text: What’s the difference between management reserves and contingency reserves?
explanation: Management reserves are evaluated by the top management to accommodate for the unforeseen risks. Contingency reserves are calculated during the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process to account for the residual risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 202 and 439 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 62

choices.text: Management reserves are used to handle residual risks, while contingency reserves are used to handle unforeseen risks.
Management reserves are used to handle unforeseen risks, while contingency reserves are used to handle residual risks.
Management reserves are used to handle secondary risks, while contingency reserves are used to handle residual risks.
Management reserves are used to handle low-priority risks, while contingency reserves are used to handle high-priority risks.

text: You are managing a construction project to build a new school. You are working with your team to develop the network diagram. A team member suggests that the quality of work could be improved if you start the electricity work before starting the plumbing work. But if it leads to increasing the schedule duration, they both can be done in parallel. What type of dependency is he referring to?
explanation: As the two activities can both be performed in parallel, so it is a discretionary dependency. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 191 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 60

choices.text: External dependency
Mandatory dependency
Discretionary dependency
Internal dependency

text: You are managing a construction project in your organization budgeted at 4,000,000$ and scheduled to be completed in 10 months. During project executing, the customer complains that the project is running behind schedule. The sponsor is holding a meeting with you and the customer. The sponsor asked you about the reason of this delay, you answered him that you send a weekly report which contains the earned value measurements of the project. The last report shows that SPI = 1.05 which means that the project is running ahead of schedule not behind schedule. The customer insists that a critical deliverable due date is missed. What is the main problem in this situation?
explanation: The true answer is that you performed some activities not on the critical path earlier than planned, and some on the critical path later than planned. The values of the activities not on the critical path are higher than those on the critical path, which makes EV>PV and so SPI>1 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: Your team is not following the communication management plan.
The customer is not following the communication management plan.
You performed some activities not on the critical path earlier than planned and some activities on the critical path later than planned.
You performed some activities on the critical path earlier than planned and some activities not on the critical path later than planned.

text: A key stakeholder has approached the project team who are working on a software project with a concern. He needs to add a new feature to the existing code. This code should be delivered within one week. The same stakeholder keeps chasing the project team with many requests during the past two weeks. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: First things first, he should follow the change control process as his requirement might have an impact on any of the project constraints. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: The team should analyze the impact of his new feature.
The team should ask the stakeholder to raise a change request.
The team should escalate the key stakeholder first to the project sponsor.
He is a key stakeholder, so his requirements should be applied.

text: You have been assigned as a project manager for a large project to develop an accounting system using a predictive life cycle. The project is expected to be finished in 6 months and is budgeted at $100,000. During project execution, you discover that some requirements have not been included in the project scope. Failure to meet these requirements may result in legal actions from the customer of the project against your company. However, implementing these requirements may increase the project schedule and budget. What should you do?
explanation: All requirements must be met, so you can request a change to implement the change first, then as a result of the change, you will get the needed time and funds. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Submit a change request to add the missed requirements to the project scope.
Do nothing, these requirements are not a part of the project scope.
This is scope creep; these requirements must be ignored.
Request additional funds and time to implement these requirements.

text: A project has just been initiated in an organization. The project team want to assess the degree of complexity within the project in order to tailor methods and practices according to the project needs. Which of the following models can they use?
explanation: Stacey matrix looks at two dimensions to determine the relative complexity of a project: (a) the relative uncertainty of the requirements for the deliverable, and (b) the relative uncertainty of the technology that will be used to create the deliverable. Based on the relative uncertainty of these dimensions, a project is considered simple, complicated, complex, or chaotic. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 165

choices.text: OSCAR
Virginia Satir
Stacey Matrix
Transition Model

text: Carol is an experienced project manager in her organization. She is managing an IT project using agile approach. A team member asks Carol about the quality control activities when and by whom they will be performed. Which of the following would be her answer?
explanation: In agile projects, the Control Quality activities may be performed by all team members throughout the project life cycle. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 75

choices.text: The Control Quality activities may be performed by all team members towards the end of the project or phase.
The Control Quality activities may be performed by Quality Control team throughout the project life cycle.
The Control Quality activities may be performed by all team members throughout the project life cycle.
The Control Quality activities may be performed by Quality Control team towards the end of the project or phase.

text: Sergio is the sponsor of a large software project. The project includes 1250 stakeholders located in three different countries. He assigned Michael as a project manager for this project. Mario, a business analyst with a good experience, is assigned to this project to conduct business analysis. Who is responsible for ensuring that requirements-related work is accounted for in the project management plan and that requirements-related activities are performed on time and within budget?
explanation: The project manager is responsible for ensuring that requirements-related work is accounted for in the project management plan and that requirements-related activities are performed on time and within budget and deliver value. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 132

choices.text: Sergio
Michael
Mario
Project team members

text: Midway through a sprint, a functional manager has withdrawn a developer form the agile team to complete some previously uncompleted tasks from another project. So, the team will not be able to meet their sprint goal. What should have been done to avoid this issue?
explanation: The team should consider the risks related to the team availability at the beginning of the sprint. Domain = Process.

choices.text: Always work fast to finish the work early and don't wait till the end of the sprint.
Reject to add any team member to the team who might have any previously uncompleted tasks.
Add buffers to estimates to account for such unknown risks.
Consider human resources availability during sprint planning.

text: A large IT project has been initiated in an organization. The project team has created the project management plan and holds a kick-off meeting to get approval from key stakeholders in order to start executing the project activities. The Value Delivery Office (VDO) objects to some parts of the project management plan as the project team postponed the engagement of finance department to a late time in the project, however they should be engaged earlier as per VDO point of view. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Tailoring that only impacts the project team (e.g., when they hold internal meetings, who works where, etc.) requires less oversight than tailoring that impacts external groups (e.g., how and when other departments are engaged, etc.). Therefore, internal project [details can be decided by the PM, but changes affecting external groups may require PMO or VDO approval].

choices.text: Consult the finance department when they would like to be engaged in the project.
Escalate this topic to the project sponsor.
VDO feedback should be considered.
It's a project team decision.

text: Which of the following activities is closely related to Agile Principle 2: "Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage"?
explanation: As expressed in Agile Manifesto Principle 2, staying flexible to meet the customer’s changing business needs is fundamental to agile, and the backlog is one of the key ways that agile teams ensure this adaptability. The backlog is always evolving, and it needs to be kept continually updated with the latest requirements and new information. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8.

choices.text: Limiting WIP
Grooming the backlog
Frequent verification and validation
Minimizing waste

text: Who should know the most about the progress toward a business objective or a release in a scrum team?
explanation: The product owner is responsible for ordering the product backlog and making it visible and transparent to all stakeholders. So, he knows most about the progress toward business objectives. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: The project sponsor
The scrum master
The product owner
The developers

text: What is the correct name of the seller in the following procurements management processes respectively: Plan Procurement Management, Conduct Procurements and Control Procurements?
explanation: The seller can be viewed during the contract life cycle first as a bidder during the Plan Procurement Management process, then as the selected source during the Conduct Procurements process, and then as the contracted supplier or vendor during the Control Procurements process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461

choices.text: Potential seller, contracted supplier and selected source
Selected bidder, potential vendor and contracted supplier
Selected seller, potential seller and contracted vendor
Bidder, selected source and contracted supplier

text: During weekly project status meeting, the project stakeholders keep asking questions like "what is the current status of the project?", "are we ahead or behind schedule?", "Did we miss this milestone or not?",..etc. What is the most probable reason for these questions?
explanation: In case of lack of visibility and understanding of project progress, check to ensure appropriate measures are being collected, analyzed, shared, and discussed during team and stakeholder meetings; validate agreement with the measures within the team and with stakeholders. The project team is not using the preferred communication method for project stakeholders has low probability, because this issue happened with many stakeholders. Having no communication management plan has low probability, because there is a weekly status meeting which is being determined and scheduled as part of the communication management plan. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: Appropriate measures are not being collected, analyzed, shared or discussed during team and stakeholder meetings.
The project stakeholders don't read the project status report before the project status meeting.
The project team don't use the preferred communication method for project stakeholders.
There is no communication management plan in place.

text: Catherine is managing a project to author a book about using earned value management in construction projects. She asked Maria, a technical writer, to help her in this project. Maria advised her to pay attentions to the following points: using correct grammar and spelling, using concise expressions and eliminating excess words, having clear purpose, using markers such as introduction and summaries of the ideas, and controlling flow of words and ideas using graphics. Which techniques did Maria advised Catherine to use?
explanation: The 5Cs of written communications involves using correct grammar and spelling, concise expression and elimination of excess words, clear purpose and expression directed to the needs of the reader, coherent logical flow of ideas and controlling flow of words and ideas. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 363

choices.text: Interactive Communication
Cultural Awareness
5C's
Data Representation

text: The same project may be assigned to two different teams, Team A has estimated 420 story points and 30 points velocity per two weeks iteration. Team B estimated 280 story points and 20 points velocity per two weeks iteration. Which of the following statements is correct?
explanation: There is no meaningful comparison of velocity “between” different teams, since such teams may have different approaches to estimation. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Team B has underestimated project scope.
Team A is more efficient than team B.
Team A didn't account for disruptions in their estimate.
None of the above

text: An agile team has completed 10 story points in the current iteration, although the team has committed to complete 15 story points per iteration. What should the team do?
explanation: The project team should complete the 10 user stories and add the remaining 5 user stories to the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 64

choices.text: Add more resources to the current iteration.
Re-estimate the team velocity.
Complete 10 story points and add remaining 5 story points to the product backlog.
Extend the current iteration.

text: You work as a project manager for a risky construction project. You and your team have identified risks, then performed the qualitative and quantitative risk analysis with the help of experts. During planning risk responses, a new team member asked you about the meaning of a residual risk. What would you tell him?
explanation: Residual risks that are expected to remain after planned responses have been taken. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 448 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: It is a risk that can not be addressed by a risk response.
It is a risk that will remain no matter what type of risk response is offered.
It is a risk that remains because no risk response is taken.
It is a risk that remains after planned risk responses are taken.

text: Joseph is a project manager for a hardware design project. The project is about to be completed when the customer refused to sign one of the last invoices saying that a user manual for the hardware must be delivered. Joseph is surprised because the user manual shouldn't be completed by his company, this happened most likely because:
explanation: The scope statement should state clearly what is as is not included in project work. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 154

choices.text: The work packages were not decomposed to project activities.
Project exclusions were not clearly identified in the project scope statement.
This requirement is not related to achieving any of the project objectives, so it was removed from the requirements traceability matrix.
This requirement was not included in the requirements management plan.

text: You are a project manager for a construction project when you discover that the local city council is about to install sewage systems. The project manager of the neighboring building adjacent to you also needs to install sewage system for his project. He discusses with you the possibility to build a sewage system for both projects together and combine the sewage system to save time and cost for both projects because you can use the same resources. Which risk response strategy will he use?
explanation: Sharing a positive risk involves allocating some or all of the ownership of the opportunity to a third party who is best able to capture the opportunity for the benefit of the two projects. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 444 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: Transfer
Share
Accept
Exploit

text: A timeboxed event means that:
explanation: Timeboxed events have a maximum duration. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 154

choices.text: The event must happen at a given time.
The event must take a minimum amount of time.
The event can't take more than a maximum amount of time.
The event should take average time plus or minus 10%.

text: The development team of an agile project has not completed any new features during the last iteration. They are going to hold a product demonstration with the customer representative and stakeholders. What should they do?
explanation: Lessons learned and why no new features were completed can be discussed during the retrospective. During product demonstration (iteration review), the team and stakeholders review what was accomplished in the iteration and collaborate on what to do next. The Product Backlog may also be adjusted to meet new opportunities. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide.

choices.text: They should discuss the user stories to be added to the next iteration.
The product demonstration should be cancelled.
They should discuss the root cause of having no new completed features during this iteration.
They should discuss the lessons learned during the last iteration.

text: A cost performance index (CPI) of 0.98 means:
explanation: A cost performance index means how much dollars you get from each dollar invested. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: At this time, we expect the total project to cost 98 percent of the planned cost.
The project is progressing at 98 percent of the rate planned.
When the project is completed, we will have spent 98 percent of the planned cost.
The project is getting 98 cents out of every dollar invested.

text: What could be a PMO role in agile transformation?
explanation: The PMO can help the organization stay on the right side of required compliance needs. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 81

choices.text: Providing guidelines to ensure compliance with agile frameworks.
Providing agile tools like Kanban boards and burn charts.
Providing project managers to new agile projects.
Ensuring compliance with standard team velocity.

text: You are a project manager for a $10,000,000 construction project. A part of the project work will be outsourced to subcontractors. The procurement department is now contacting subcontractors to obtain bids and proposals. Which of the following techniques would help you save time because you know that it is a time-consuming procedure to ensure that the sellers get a clear and common understanding of the procurement process and of the needs of your project?
explanation: Bidder conferences are used to ensure that all prospective sellers have a clear and common understanding of the procurement requirements. Also you answer all sellers' questions. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 487 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 70

choices.text: Bidder conferences
Advertising
Qualified sellers list
Proposal evaluation

text: You are a project manager for a complex software project. You need to assign a web developer to your project to perform some complex activities. The needed developer should have great experience to perform the needed activities. You asked the resource manager to assign Steve, who is known that he is an excellent web developer in the organization and he has great experience. The resource manager rejected because Steve is assigned full time to another higher priority project in the organization. You have a conflict with the resource manager because assigning any other developer to perform these activities has great risk to your project, because all other developers within the organization have little experience. Which process are you involved in?
explanation: You are involved in the Acquire Resources process, because the project manager here negotiates with the resource manager to provide the required resources for the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 330

choices.text: Manage Team
Acquire Resources
Estimate Activity Resources
Control Resources

text: You are a project manager for a new project in a weak matrix organization. The project team formally don’t report to you and you don’t have authority to reward them for their performance. The project is very difficult and has aggressive schedule and tight date constraints. The project requires a high quality standard. Based on these circumstances, what is the most effective power for the project manager will be likely to use?
explanation: The project manager works in a weak matrix organization, so all the powers derived from his position will not be effective like reward, penalty or formal powers. The only power he can use here is the expert power. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

choices.text: Reward
Penalty
Formal
Expert

text: Midway through the iteration, a major organization change is about to take place and the team members don't have a clear vision about the future direction of the product. The team members feel that the iteration should be placed on hold till more clarity about the future of the product emerges. Who should take this decision?
explanation: The product owner is the one who has the authority to cancel the iteration or put it on hold. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: The product owner
The agile coach
The team
The project sponsor

text: Marcos is managing a large construction project. During project execution, he finds that the floor plans received from the customer seem to be incomplete. What should Marcos do first?
explanation: The best solution is to resolve the conflict in a direct collaborative approach. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 348 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Raise a change request to extend the project end date to accommodate the expected delay.
Escalate this topic to the project sponsor.
Use the floor plan provided by the customer as the customer is accountable to provide the correct floor plan.
Consult with the customer to determine if the project execution should be continued.

text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto principles is more related to prioritizing value?
explanation: Prioritization is a fundamental agile process. If the customer has been continuously prioritizing the backlog, then any new requirement is simply inserted at the appropriate place in the backlog. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 52

choices.text: Working software is the primary measure of progress.
Deliver working software frequently, from a couple of weeks to a couple of months, with a preference to the shorter timescale.
Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage.
Our highest priority is to satisfy the customer through early and continuous delivery of valuable software.

text: Which of the following would not be appropriate to mention during the daily scrum?
explanation: During daily standup the scrum team discuss what did I complete since the last standup? What am I planning to complete between now and the next standup? And What are my impediments (or risks or problems)? An antipattern typically seen in standups is that the team begins to solve problems as they become apparent. Standups are for realizing there are problems—not for solving them.

choices.text: The new software saved much time as it automated the reporting process.
I have successfully activated two sites.
We are trying to fix the bug discovered yesterday which caused game crash.
The customer contact is out of office this week which will delay the current activity till next week.

text: Testing the stability of one of the electronic chips manufactured by your company to determine if it meets your quality standards is an example of:
explanation: Statistical sampling involves choosing part of a population of interest for inspection. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 303

choices.text: Benchmarking
Design of experiments
Statistical sampling
Design for X

text: Alex is managing a construction project when two team members come to him with a conflict. A civil engineer has identified an important project risk: there is a subcontractor that may not deliver work on time. The designer believes that the risk has a low probability of occurring; however, he consults the lessons learned from previous projects and discovers that the subcontractor failed to deliver work on three previous projects. The civil engineer suggests that two team members take one week to evaluate the possibility of doing this work in-house and come up with some initial work that they can do in case the subcontractor does not deliver. Alex decides to follow the civil engineer’s suggestion over the objections of the designer. Which risk response strategy did he use?
explanation: Mitigation means reducing probability or impact. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123.

choices.text: Transference
Mitigation
Avoidance
Acceptance

text: A three phases project adopts a hybrid development approach. The first phase is hardware development adopts a traditional approach. The second phase is software development adopts an adaptive approach. The third phase is systems integration adopts an adaptive approach. The first phase has come to an end. The project manager and the project team are ready to start the second phase. Accordingly some roles and responsibilities will change. Which of the following responsibilities will be changed for the project manager?
explanation: The project manager's role with a predictive phase is more directive and controlling, whereas agile leadership requires servant leadership. Agile teams are considered self-organizing. The team will plan and control the project work. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 41

choices.text: Detailed work planning, product delivery and team management will be delegated to the project team.
Planning the project work through Kanban board instead of Gantt chart.
Act as a servant leader helping the team sizing the user stories and calculating the team's velocity.
Act as a team facilitator by helping the team to assign user stories according to the team members' skills.

text: You are an agile practitioner working on a product development project. A stakeholder asks you about the remaining features to be delivered. What should you do in this situation?
explanation: Information radiators are usually displayed in high-traffic areas to maximize exposure,where they can quickly inform stakeholders about the project’s status. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 108

choices.text: Ask him to contact the product owner.
Send him the burndown chart.
Guide him to the information radiator.
Apologize to him and send a detailed report about the project status including remaining features to be delivered.

text: Midway through the project, the project manager discovers that he has missed some risks during project planning. The project is now being executed, and the project management plan has been approved. Which of the following actions should the project manager do regarding the new risks?
explanation: As new risks are identified, they should be assessed, and risk responses should be planned. If they need extra budget or time to address them, they should be requested through integrated change control. Risk identification is an ongoing process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59.

choices.text: Re-evaluate the risk identification process.
The new risks might happen or not; identify them and don't request additional budget or time until they actually occur.
Address them through management reserve as they were unforeseen during risk identification.
Evaluate the risks, follow the change control process, and re-baseline.

text: Contingency reserves are often viewed as the part of the budget intended to address:
explanation: Contingency reserves are often viewed as the part of the budget intended to address the known unknowns that can affect a project. The certain events are facts not risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 245 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 62

choices.text: Anticipated and certain events
Anticipated and uncertain events
Unanticipated events
Scope Creep

text: George is responsible for managing the spare parts in a large tele-communication project. He monitors the spare parts usage in a timely manner. He always informs the project manager if any spare part has shortage or surplus and takes the needed actions to adjust spare parts usage. Which process is he involved in?
explanation: Control Resources is concerned with identifying and dealing with resource shortage/surplus in a timely manner. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 354

choices.text: Monitor Risks
Acquire Resources
Control Resources
Control Scope

text: You are a project manager for a large software project to develop a mobile application. Your project team consists of 25 persons located in the same office. In the middle of the project, you noticed that conflicts arise between two project team members on both technical and interpersonal levels. What should you do to handle these conflicts?
explanation: No need to solve a conflict between two team members in a team meeting. The issue should be handled between the two team members only. Solving the root cause of the problem is better than withdrawal or forcing. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 169

choices.text: Postpone handling these conflicts whenever possible to allow those involved in the conflict to calm down which makes solving conflict easier.
Handle the conflict in the project team meeting so that the entire team can participate in finding a solution.
Address the conflict in private using a direct, collaborative approach.
Use your power to push the correct point of view to focus on achieving the project objectives.

text: Rayan is a junior developer who has just joined an agile team. He has never worked before in agile environment. He asks the agile coach about how to calculate the project schedule in agile projects. Which of the following represents the best agile coach answer?
explanation: In agile projects, schedule is calculated by estimating story points and applying team velocity. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 23

choices.text: Calculating the critical path duration.
Estimating story points and applying team velocity.
Timeboxing the iterations.
Analyzing activities and determining dependencies.

text: A new team member asks the project manager about the correct order of actions that will be taken during the Close Project or Phase process. What would be his answer?
explanation: You will need your team members help during documenting lessons learned, so you can't release them before documenting lessons learned. the last thing you do on the project is close the contract. The reason for this is that you don’t want to have to wait for payment before releasing the team, because most contracts have payment terms that allow for some period of time before full payment is required. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Obtain formal acceptance, release the team members, document lessons learned, close the contract
Document lessons learned, release the team members, obtain formal acceptance, close the contract
Obtain formal acceptance, document lessons learned, release the team members, close the contract
Obtain formal acceptance, close the contract, document lessons learned, release the team members
