text: You are managing a network upgrade project. Your project schedule performance index is 0.79. You need to take a corrective action to bring the project back on track. Which response would you choose that will affect the resources management plan?
explanation: Crashing is a technique used to shorten the schedule duration for the least incremental cost by adding resources to critical path activities. Adding resources will update the resources management plan to reflect the rates, roles and calendars for the added resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Crashing
Fast Tracking
Teaming Agreement
Sharing

text: Midway during an iteration, one of the project team members informs the project leader that his computer is not connected to the company intranet. What should the project leader do?
explanation: The project leader is a team facilitator and a servant leader. Servant leaders work to fulfill the needs of the teams, projects, and organization. Servant leader focuses on paving the way for the team to do its best work. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 36

choices.text: The team is cross functional, ask another team member who may have IT experience to step in and fix the issue.
Facilitate a resolution with IT department.
The team is self organized, the team member should try to resolve the issue by himself or contact IT department.
Update the issue log and develop a resolution plan.

text: You are a project manager for a large infrastructure project. Your project includes civil, electric and mechanical work. Your organization doesn't have the adequate experience in all these fields, so you will need to outsource some work to more experienced companies. So you need to start with your team planning for procurement management. When during the project life cycle of the project you perform the Plan Procurement Management process?
explanation: This Plan Procurement Management process is performed once or at predefined points in the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 466

choices.text: At the beginning of the project
Throughout the project life cycle
Once or at pre-defined points in the project
At the beginning of each phase of the project

text: Which of the following statements best describes the difference between a project charter and a contract?
explanation: A project charter can't be considered as a contract. A project charter is not sufficient when performing a project for an external organization, a contract must be signed. Correct answer is that project charter establishes a partnership between the performing and requesting organization. For external project a formal contract is the preferred way to establish an agreement. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 77 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 46

choices.text: A project charter can be considered as a contract because it initiates and authorizes a project.
A project charter is sufficient when performing a project for an external customer.
A contract is issued by the project initiator or sponsor to formally authorize a project.
A project charter establishes a partnership between the performing and requesting organization. For external project a formal contract is the preferred way to establish an agreement.

text: A small project has been initiated in ABC organization which is known to be working on mega projects. The organization adopts waterfall approach for all projects and has strict project management processes. The new project team consists of four colocated team members. The project manager decides not to create some artifacts like communication management plan, meeting minutes, stakeholders engagement plan and quality management plan. Which of the following best describes what the project manager is doing?
explanation: Tailoring is the deliberate adaptation of the project management approach, governance, and processes to make them more suitable for the given environment and the work at hand. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 131

choices.text: Violating the PMI processes
Tailoring
Noncompliance
Creating new approach

text: During a weekly project status meeting, you ask one of the stakeholders if he reviewed the document you sent him last week. He said: “I’m not sure, I don’t know what you are talking about”. Which engagement level is this stakeholder displaying?
explanation: Here the situation indicates that the person doesn't know anything about his role and responsibility in the project, so he is unaware. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 521

choices.text: Unaware
Resistant
Supportive
Leading

text: Which of the following statements represents a fixed price incentive fee (FPIF) contract?
explanation: To pay only $1,500,000, it is a FFP contract not FPIP. To pay all the costs plus something, it is a CR contract. The only correct answer is to pay a fixed amount plus bonus if a certain criteria is achieved. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 471 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: Pay the costs plus $15,000 for every month the project is finished earlier than July 15.
Pay $1,500,000 plus $15,000 for every designated incremental quality level reached.
Pay all the costs plus a $15,000 fee.

text: William is a project manager in a chemicals industrial company. The PMO in this company has authorized William to manage a project to lessen environmental impacts of one of its products. He found that replacing one type of material used in the product with another new environment friendly material, will decrease environmental impacts. He and his team are studying the consequences of removing the material and using the new material on the product technical properties like quality and price. Which technique should help the team in this situation?
explanation: A scatter diagram is a graph that shows the relationship between two variables. Scatter diagrams can demonstrate a relationship between any element of a process, environment, or activity on one axis and a quality defect on the other axis. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 293 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: Matrix diagrams
Cause- and- effect diagram
Scatter diagram
Histogram

text: An organization has adopted an agile approach recently for a new project. The project sponsor directs their project manager, who has been assigned as a team facilitator, to add a new high-priority feature to the product. How should the project manager handle this scope change?
explanation: It is the ultimate responsibility of the product owner to manage and prioritize the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 52.

choices.text: The project manager should add the new feature to the product backlog and make it the top priority.
On an agile project, changes are welcomed at any time, so this feature should be assigned to the team for development.
Communicate the new feature request to the product owner to be considered in the next backlog session.
Tell the sponsor to talk with the product owner about this feature, reminding them that the product owner has authority to prioritize features.

text: A project team is working on a project to build a part of a nuclear reactor using waterfall development approach. The project team wants to tailor some processes related to reporting and communication by removing some activities which seem to be not necessary. The performing organization senior management object to this tailoring approach and added additional approval layer for the project team before changing any process. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Additional constraints for tailoring for the organization include large, safety-critical projects. Large, safety-critical project tailoring suggestions may require additional oversight and approval to help prevent errors, loss, or subsequent issues. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 139

choices.text: Tailoring the project documents or processes should be the project team decision not the senior management decision.
The project senior management are overstepping their role.
Tailoring any project documents or processes should be agreed with the senior management in advance.
This project has tailoring constraints.

text: What is the status of the project when the earned value of the project is higher than both the actual costs and planned value?
explanation: When the EV is greater than both PV and AC then this means that the value of all the work completed EV to this point in time is greater than the value of the work planned to be completed PV and the actual costs spent AC. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project is performing good as it is running faster than planned and under the planned cost.
The project is not performing good as it is running slower than planned and under the planned cost.
The project is not performing good as it is running slower than planned and over the planned cost.
The project is not performing good as it is running faster than planned and over the planned cost.

text: An IT project has been initiated in XYZ organization. The project is facing high complexity due to linking the organization system to the customer system. The two systems are very complex and the project team can't make accurate predections about the likelihoodof potential outcome. Which of the following might help the project team in this situation?
explanation: Working with complexity that is systems based include decoupling. Decoupling entails disconnecting parts of the system to both simplify the system and reduce the number of connected variables. Determining how a piece of a system works on its own reduces the overall size of the problem. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 121

choices.text: Prototypes
Add contingency reserve
Decoupling
Progressive elaboration

text: An organization adopts agile transformation. Some project managers roles will be switched to team facilitators according to the new structure. They will need to enhance servant leadership skills. Which of the following is an example of servant leadership?
explanation: Servant leadership focuses on removing impediments and supporting the team to perform at its best. Doing administrative work for the team is an example of servant leadership. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide

choices.text: Creating the project plan on weekend so as not to disturb the team.
Doing the administrative work for the team.
Posting the project Gantt chart on the office wall.
Handing out detailed task lists to the team.

text: Andy is a project manager for a large construction project. All the project work will be outsourced to other subcontractors. Andy is just starting the procurement process. Which of the following should be done last?
explanation: Performing make-or-buy analysis is done firstly during planning process group in the Plan Procurement Management process, then seeking experts judgement and then selecting the contract type. But contract terms and conditions is done later in the Conduct Procurement process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 489 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 74–77

choices.text: Seek expert judgment from a variety of resources.
Select contract type whether FP, T and M or CR.
Perform make-or-buy analysis.
Finalize the terms and conditions of the contract.

text: You are a project manager for a large industrial project. To test your product you need a special equipment. You need to decide whether to buy or lease the equipment. The daily lease cost is $150. The cost of purchasing the item is $2,000 investment cost & $50 daily cost. You need to use the item for about 12 days. What should you do?
explanation: lease 12*150 = 1800 < 2000+(12*50). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 473 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 65

choices.text: Lease it
Consult the sponsor
Buy it
Ask the customer to pay for $2,000 change order

text: In the middle of a sprint, a team member is out on a sick leave. The sprint is very critical and any delay in the product increment delivery would endanger the sprint goal. The scrum team are working on their full capacity. In light of this, what should the scrum master do?
explanation: It is true that the sprint can not be extended, but doing nothing is not an appropriate solution. Working overtime is against agile manifesto eighth principle which states that the team should maintain sustainable pace. The best solution is to share the team challenge with the stakeholders and the project sponsor. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide

choices.text: Ask the scrum team to work overtime till the team member is back from his sick leave.
Discuss this topic with the sponsor and stakeholders in order to share the team challenge.
Ask the team to redistribute the tasks between them and focus only on the most important features.
Do nothing, the sprint can not be extended.

text: All of the following may be included in project business case, except:
explanation: The expected tangible and intangible value to be gained by the implementation of the project is one of the contents of the project benefits management plan. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 31 and 32

choices.text: Gap analysis of capabilities needed for the project versus existing capabilities of the organization.
Identification of critical success factors.
The expected tangible and intangible value to be gained by the implementation of the project.
Statement describing the plan for measuring benefits the project will deliver.

text: What is meant by self-organizing teams?
explanation: The self-organizing team, where the team functions with an absence of centralized control. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 310 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 249

choices.text: The team consists of subject matter experts.
The team functions without a team leader.
The team functions without conflicts between team members.
The team functions with an absence of centralized control.

text: Stephen and Ann are two stakeholders are discussing about the impact of the project on their departments. They both were not satisfied with the impacts. Stephen asked Ann if the project is on budget. Ann informed him that CPI is 1.03. Stephen and Ann agreed that they are not sure that the project manager knows their concerns. The project manager heard this conversation between them. What is the best thing he can do?
explanation: Directing any questions to the project manager in writing is not the best choice. Make a presentation to all the stakeholders regarding the status of the project is not a good solution because only two stakeholders have this problem. The issue log is the record where stakeholders' problems with the project are recorded, but here the project manager is unaware of the reservations of both stakeholders regarding the project. Consequently, using the issue log is not the best solution. It is better to arrange a meeting with both stakeholders to learn about their reservations.

choices.text: Send both stakeholders a copy of the issue log and ask for additional comments.
Ask both stakeholders to direct any questions to the project manager in writing.
Make a presentation to all the stakeholders regarding the status of the project.
Arrange a meeting with both stakeholders to allow them to voice any concerns they may have.

text: You are managing a large software project that’s been broken down into sub-projects (or phases). Each of these sub-projects is scheduled to take between four and seven months to complete. At the end of each sub-project, you plan to go through the closing processes and document lessons learned. Which of the following best describes what you must do at the beginning of each sub-project or phase?
explanation: You should identify stakeholders at the beginning of each phase. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: Make sure that each team member knows the activities assigned to him.
Identify the stakeholders.
Make sure that performance measurement baselines are ready for the next phase.
Release all resources from the project and contact sellers to renegotiate all contracts.

text: Sergio is the sponsor of a large software project. The project includes 1250 stakeholders located in three different countries. He assigned Michael as a project manager for this project. Mario, a business analyst with a good experience, is assigned to this project to conduct business analysis. Who is responsible for requirements related activities in this project?
explanation: If a business analyst is assigned to a project, requirement-related activities are the responsibility of that role. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 132

choices.text: Sergio
Michael
Mario
Project team members

text: Ricardo is managing a software project to produce a mobile application. The product of the project has ten main functionalities as per the customer requirements. Ricardo agreed with the customer that the project will be performed in five months and 12,000 $ budget. They agreed that the product of the project will be produced through a series of iterations that successively add required functionalities within the predetermined time frame. The product will be considered complete after adding the final functionality. Which type of project life cycle is used here?
explanation: In an incremental life cycle, the deliverable is produced through a series of iterations that successively add functionality within a predetermined time frame. The deliverable contains the necessary and sufficient capability to be considered complete only after the final iteration. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 19 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 36

choices.text: Waterfall
Iterative
Incremental
Adaptive

text: A project has been initiated several months ago to build a new factory. The project has been postponed several times due to non-compliance with environmental codes required by local laws in the country where the project is being performed. A new project manager has been assigned to this project. What should the new project manager do to move forward and have this project completed?
explanation: Projects must comply with laws, rules, regulations, and requirements that are properly authorized within or outside of their organization. However, high-performing projects seek ways to integrate compliance more fully into the project culture, creating more alignment with diverse and potentially conflicting guidelines. So, the best approach is to include environmental compliance requirements in the project scope. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 27

choices.text: Meet with the city council representatives, explain to them the project objectives and the benefits of building this factory to the city.
Include environmental compliance requirements in the project scope, identify risks to achieving these requirements and develop responses.
Consult lessons learned collected by previous project managers, include enough cost and schedule reserves to accommodate for any further delays or penalties.
Descope the non-compliant requirements to move forward.

text: Net Promoter Score can be used to measure:
explanation: A Net Promoter Score measures the degree to which a stakeholder is willing to recommend a product or service to others. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 103

choices.text: Stakeholders Satisfaction
Business Value
Deviation from Baselines
Deliverables Attributes

text: What is the difference between risk audit and risk reviews?
explanation: Risk audits are a type of audit that may be used to consider the effectiveness of the risk management process. The project manager is responsible for ensuring that risk audits are performed at an appropriate frequency, as defined in the project’s risk management plan. Risk reviews are scheduled regularly and should examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses in dealing with overall project risk and with identified individual project risks. Risk reviews may also result in identification of new individual project risks, reassessment of current risks and the closing of risks that are outdated. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456 and 457

choices.text: They are the same.

Risk audits are used to consider the effectiveness of the risk management process, while risk reviews should examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses in dealing with overall project risk and with identified individual project risks.

Risk audits result in identification of new individual project risks, reassessment of current risks, and the closing of risks that are outdated, while risk reviews are used to consider the effectiveness of the risk management process.

The risk manager is responsible for ensuring that risk audits are performed at an appropriate frequency, as defined in the project’s risk management plan. While, the project manager is responsible for conducting risk reviews meetings.

text: Which of the following statements is correct regarding inspection and audit during the Control Procurement process?
explanation: An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the contractor. This may involve a simple review of the deliverables or an actual physical review of the work itself. While audits are a structured review of the procurement process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498

choices.text: An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the contractor, while audits are a structured review of the procurement process.
An inspection is a structured review of the payments done by the buyer, while audits are a structured review of the procurement process.
An inspection is performed by the seller, while audits are performed by the buyer.
An inspection is a structured review of the procurement process, while audits are a structured review of the work being performed by the buyer.

text: Andrew is a project manager in an industrial firm. He is taking over a manufacturing project from another project manager who resigned two weeks ago. Previously the company has faced criticism for the project deliverables from the customer. The previous project manager didn't pay much attention to quality of deliverables. Andrew hired inspectors to inspect the product of the project and prevent the defective ones from reaching the customer. He required from the extra inspectors to record the inspection results of the products as either conform or do not conform to quality requirements. This technique is known as:
explanation: Attribute sampling means that the result either conforms or does not conform and variable sampling means that the result is rated on a continuous scale that measures the degree of conformity. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 274

choices.text: Variable sampling
Attribute sampling
Prevention
Statistical sampling

text: Two team members are arguing about the design of new network they are building. One has experience from a prior project and argues for a wireless design; the other is concerned about the business risk and wants a more sophisticated wired design. The second team member asks you to resolve the disagreement, implying that the other team member doesn't have enough experience to create the more complex wired network design. As the project manager, how should you approach this conflict?
explanation: The team members should try to resolve the conflict themselves first. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Tell them they need to resolve their technical differences between themselves.
Report the team members to human resources for disrespectful behavior toward each other.
Explain their different communication styles to help them understand each other better.
Have one of the team members transferred to another team, since they obviously don't get along.

text: You are a project manager for a large software project in a multinational organizai­on. Your project consists of 370 stakeholders located in three countries. In the middle of the project, the customer has requested a change to the project scope. You have evaluated the impacts of the change and found that it will add one week to the project critical path and 3,000$ to the project budget. You want to document the impacts of the change. Which communications method would you use in this situation?
explanation: Here you are dealing with large and international audience, so pull communication is more effective. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: Push communication
Pull communication
Verbal communication
Interactive communication

text: Midway through the project, the project manager discovers that he has missed some risks during project planning. The project is now being executed, and the project management plan has been approved. Which of the following actions should the project manager do regarding the new risks?
explanation: As new risks are identified, they should be assessed, and risk responses should be planned. If they need extra budget or time to address them, they should be requested through integrated change control. Risk identification is an ongoing process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59.

choices.text: Re-evaluate the risk identification process.
The new risks might happen or not; identify them and don't request additional budget or time until they actually occur.
Address them through management reserve as they were unforeseen during risk identification.
Evaluate the risks, follow the change control process, and re-baseline.

text: Patrick is a project manager who had previous agile experience. His organization - which adopts traditional approach - has contracted with ABC company to develop a website and assigned Patrick as a project manager from the organization's side. The ABC company is using agile approach to develop the website. How can Patrick assess their progress?
explanation: During iteration review (product demo) the team demonstrates all completed work items at the end of the iteration. It is better for Patrick to see the completed work himself instead of sending reports. As per agile Manifesto that working product is the primary measure of progress. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 55

choices.text: Attend the daily standup with the project team of ABC company.
Ask the agile coach in ABC company to send him weekly progress reports.
Review iteration plans of the development team in ABC company.
Attend the iteration reviews to review released versions of the required work.

text: Robert has been promoted to a project manager position in his organization. He will be assigned to manage the next project. The human resources policy in the organization states that the maximum number of human resources to work on any project should not exceed ten human resources. He must comply with this policy while developing the project schedule. This is an example of:
explanation: Resource smoothing is a technique that adjusts the activities of a schedule model such that the requirements for resources on the project do not exceed certain predefined resource limits. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 211

choices.text: Resource Smoothing
Resource Leveling
Pre-assignment
Organizational Theory

text: Frank is managing a major mechanical project using a predictive approach. The customer requested a change to the schedule baseline. Frank has determined the effect on the scope and cost, performed integrated change control, and adjusted the project baselines, the project management plan, and project documents. What should he do next?
explanation: The change has already been evaluated and approved; the next step is to inform stakeholders affected by the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Look for impacts to quality.
Submit the change to the Change Control Board for approval.
Look for other changes from the stakeholders.
Notify stakeholders affected by the change.

text: Junior is the project manager for a large construction project. The project is scheduled to be performed in two years and budgeted at 20,000,000 $. He and his team have divided the project into four phases. They planned the first phase in details and defined the needed activities to be performed during this phase, while there is not enough information to define activities to be performed in the later phases. So, they planned the later phases at a higher level until more information becomes available. Which technique are they using?
explanation: Rolling wave planning is an iterative planning technique in which the work to be accomplished in the near term is planned in detail, while work further in the future is planned at a higher level. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 185

choices.text: Decomposition
Dependency Determination
Rolling Wave Planning
Precedence Diagramming Method

text: You are a project manager of a seven-month construction project. The project is very important to the customer and your organization. You have held weekly meetings with your project customer and key stakeholders. After six months of work, the project is on schedule and budget, but the customer is not satisfied with the project deliverables. This situation may delay the project completion date by two months. Which of the following is the most important process that could have prevented this situation?
explanation: The question here is asking about the process that could have prevented this situation. If the project scope, product scope and project deliverables were defined in details during the Define Scope process, it would prevent this situation to happen. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 154 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 84

choices.text: Define Scope
Control Risks
Control Schedule
Control Scope

text: You are a project manager for a major construction project. The customer is very angry because there is a deliverable due date within two weeks and he believes that your team will not complete the deliverable before the due date. You meet with the customer to reduce his stress and you show him the earned value calculations. They show your cost performance index (CPI) is 1.25, your actual cost (AC) is $400 million, and your planned value (PV) is $490 million. Should your customer be comfortable?
explanation: Since CPI = EV/ AC, then EV= 1.25 400 M= 500 M, so SV= EV-PV = 500-490= 10M, SPI= EV/PV =500/490= 1.02, therefore the project is ahead of schedule, but this doesn’t mean that the activities needed to deliver project deliverables are on schedule, these deliverables might not be on the critical path. So he needs to prove to him that the activities leading to this deliverable are on schedule. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100*

choices.text: Yes, your SPI is 1.05.
Yes, your SV is $10 million.
No, your CPI is too high.
He needs more data.

text: Victor is a project manager working on a project to create a mobile application for a customer. The project team consists of 10 experienced developers who thrive on autonomy. The team has low morale and feel demotivated. What can Victor do to tackle this issue?
explanation: Project team members who thrive on autonomy will perform better if they can establish their own ways of working and even their own work hours and cadence. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 25

choices.text: Request a salary increase for the team.
Stretch the team goals.
Form dynamic working group for demotivated team members.
Let the team establish their own ways of working.

text: Prior to the iteration review, after the team has completed all the story points in the iteration backlog, a new industry standard has been released and the performing organization must comply with it. Accordingly, the product increment launch will not be done as planned. Which of the following is the best course of action to realize benefits?
explanation: They should review the target benefits first which will be impacted by the new standard. Understanding the organization and the industry requirements that an organization needs to satisfy allows for choosing the right conversations, the right tradeoffs, and, especially, the right techniques. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 76

choices.text: Extend current iteration to add the new standard requirements.
Review target benefits.
Release the current product increment without adding the standard requirements and add it after product demo.
Increase the team velocity to add the new requirement to the current iteration.

text: Due to recent changes in the organization structure, three persons from your project team are going to leave the organization because their positions have become redundant. This has negatively impacted the team members' morale, as the project is progressing well. What should you do as a project manager in this situation?
explanation: The process for leading change for transforming organizations is a top-down approach where the need for and approach to change originates at the top levels of the organization, and then is promoted down through the organization's layers of management to the change recipients. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 162

choices.text: Inform the team members that this is the organization's board decision.
Meet with the project team members to discuss the changes and the reasons why it has been undertaken.
Escalate to the project sponsor and let him know how the organization's decision has negatively impacted your project.
Arrange a farewell party for the released team members.

text: Stakeholder register can include all of the following, except:
explanation: Current and desired engagement levels of each stakeholder are included in the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 514 and 521 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 185

choices.text: Name, organizational position, location and role on the project of each stakeholder.
Major requirements, expectations and potential for influencing project outcomes.
Current and desired engagement levels of each stakeholder.
Stakeholder classification whether each stakeholder is Internal/external, impact/influence/ power/interest, upward/downward/ outward/sideward.

text: You are a project manager for a construction project. You are in planning process group and estimating with team members the costs needed to perform the project. The cost of the material pessimistically $120,000, most likely $110,000, and optimistically $100,000. The cost of labor is pessimistically $100,000, most likely $90,000, and optimistically $85,000. The cost of equipment is pessimistically $60,000, optimistically $40,000 and most likely $50,000. The cost for management fees is pessimistically $50,000, most likely $40,000, and optimistically $30,000. What is the probability of the project coming in under $280,000?
explanation: Average Expected Cost = (P+4M+O)/6. Average cost of material = 110,000, labor = 90,833, Equipments = 50,000, Management fees = 40,000. Average Total Cost = 110000+ 90833+50000+40000 = 290833. Average Total Cost = 110000+ 90833+50000+40000 = 290,833. Mean is at 290833 which means that the probability of total cost to be > 290833 = 50% and the probability of total cost to be < 290833 = 50%. So the probability of total cost to be < 280000$ is less than 50%. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 245

choices.text: Exact 50 %.
Less than 50%.
Over 50%.
There is not enough information.

text: A new cell phone has been released in markets as a product of a project in a multi-national organization. It seems that some defects have been noticed by customers in the new cell phone. The organization decided to make a survey in all shops to collect data about the defects. The cost regarding defects identified in the surveys may be considered as:
explanation: The cost regarding defects identified in the surveys may be considered external failure costs in the COQ model and can have extensive cost implications for the organization. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 303 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 88

choices.text: Prevention costs
Appraisal costs
Internal failure costs
External failure costs

text: Frank is managing a major construction project valued at $ 30,000,000. The project SPI is 1.01and CPI is 0.99. The sponsor called him to express concern about the project. He is worried because the project end date is after two weeks. He doesn’t know the status of the project. Frank informed him that his team sends a weekly status report by email. He told him that he doesn't prefer emails because he receives more than 500 emails per day. He doesn't check all emails and he prefers verbal updates. This situation indicates a problem in which project management process?
explanation: The Plan Communication Management process includes determining the stakeholders communication requirements which includes the preferred communication method for each stakeholder. When he informed the project sponsor that his team sends weekly project status report via email, so they are following the communication management plan. So the communication management plan doesn't reflect stakeholders communication requirements. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377

choices.text: Monitor Communications
Plan Communications Management
Manage Stakeholder Engagement
Manage Communications

text: Which of the following statements represents guilt-based power?
explanation: Imposition of obligation or sense of duty are examples of using guilt-based. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

choices.text: You must do what I say because I'm the project manager and I'm accountable on project success.
If you don't achieve your target, you will not get the annual bonus.
You must comply with HR rules and come to work on time.
If you face this type of problems in your work, solve it like this, I have been solving this type of problems the same way over the last three years and never failed.

text: The tenth principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done is essential". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: In order not to build unnecessary features that will not add value to the customer, so teams will need to boil down the requirements to their essential elements only. Backlog grooming (refinement) means continuously updating and prioritizing features into the backlog of work to be done according to their business values. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 52 and 80

choices.text: Backlog refinement
Timeboxing
Continuous integration
Test-Driven Development (TDD)

text: Which of the following is the best thing to do at bidder conference?
explanation: If sellers' questions aren't asked, it adds risks. These conferences are used to ensure that all prospective bidders have a clear and common understanding of the procurement. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 487 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 70

choices.text: Make sure the sellers have all the procurement documents.
Meet with each seller separately.
Answer sellers' questions.
Make sure the sellers ask all their questions.

text: You have a meeting with potential sponsors to select between three projects. Project A has a net present value of $64,350 and will take seven months to complete. Project B has a net present value of $92,100 and will take three years to complete. Project C has a net present value of $35,000 and a benefit-cost ratio of 5:3. Which project should you select?
explanation: Select highest NPV regardless number of years. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 34 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

choices.text: Project A
Project B
Project C
There is not enough information to decide

text: A Value Delivery Office (VDO) most probably exists in which of the following organizations?
explanation: A VDO may be found in organizations that use more adaptive delivery approaches. Only Scrum is adaptive approach all others are predictive approaches. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 141

choices.text: DEF organization which is specialized in large mechanical projects which require much up front planning.
UVW organization which adopts Scrum methodology in all its projects.
ABC construction firm which adheres to waterfall approach in all its projects.
XYZ organization which is specialized in safety critical projects.

text: Costs of testing and inspections are examples of:
explanation: Appraisal costs are costs related to evaluating, measuring, auditing, and testing the products, deliverables, or services of the specific project like testing and inspection. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 283 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 88

choices.text: Failure costs
Cost of nonconformance
Appraisal cost
Prevention cost

text: Which of the following statements is not correct?
explanation: The most efficient way to convey information to and within the project team is face-to-face communication. It transfers the most information in a given period of time. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: We value individuals and interactions over processes and tools. So, agile projects prefer face-to-face communication.
We value customer collaboration over contract negotiation. However, procurement in agile projects is always challenging as scope can't be fully defined prior to contract signature.
We value working software over comprehensive documentation. However, comprehensive documentation is the most efficient method of conveying information within the project team.
We value responding to change over following a plan. However planning an agile project is critical to project success.

text: A large project is being initiated in an organization. The project will be associated with large changes which will impact many departments within the organization. All of the following models can be used by the project manager to transition from the current to the future desired state, except:
explanation: Change models include ADKAR, Virginia Satir, transition model and the 8-step process for leading a change. Cynefin model is a complexity model. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 161-164

choices.text: ADKAR
Virginia Satir
Transition Model
Cynefin

text: You are a project manager for a new software project to develop a website for a customer. The customer wants the project to be completed in 40 days. The project is going well and the cost performance index CPI = 1.07 and the project is being performed with four team members, the project critical path duration is 38 days with a standard deviation of two days. What is the maximum project float?
explanation: Mean= 38, the project will take 38+/- 2, range 36 to 40. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Four days
Zero days
Two days
One day

text: The cost baseline is the approved version of the time-phased project budget, excluding any management reserves. It is used as a basis for comparison to actual results. It is displayed in the form of:
explanation: The cost baseline is displayed in the form of S-Curve. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 254 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: S-Curve
Inverted S-Curve
Bubble Chart
Hierarchical Chart

text: The project manager assigned Mark to send a weekly email to the project key stakeholders to explain the status of the project. Some stakeholders informed the project manager that they don't receive this email. The project manager wants to know if this email is delivered to these stakeholders or not. He asked Mark to use the option of requesting a delivery receipt from the email software which they use. Mark did what was requested from him and received notifications with who received this email. What did Mark receive?
explanation: Acknowledge means upon receipt of a message, the receiver may signal (acknowledge) receipt of the message, but this does not necessarily mean agreement with or comprehension of the message—merely that it has been received. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 373

choices.text: Feedback
Acknowledge
Noise
Response

text: Frank is managing a pipeline installation project. He and his team have finished the design of the pipeline system. The design was the first deliverable in the project. He is holding a weekly status meeting. One of the team members indicated that they need to get a permit from the city council before they start digging. The team member feels that there is a risk that a city council representative will ask them for a permit for digging. And they will need to request a change for the project management plan to add the extra time and cost needed for that permit. And if they request that change, there is a high probability that the change control board would approve the change. What is the best action to take next?
explanation: The first thing you should do is document it in the risk register, then you analyze the impact and probability of the risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411

choices.text: Use the contingency reserve to account for that risk.
Schedule a meeting with the city council representative to discuss the risk.
Add the risk to the risk register and gather information about its probability and impact.
Add the risk to the watchlist and revisit it periodically.

text: Frank is a project manager for a large construction project. During project execution, he faces shortage in some raw material which is urgently needed, as the project might face delays if these material are not available within one week. This might lead to missing important milestones and the project will be subjected to financial penalties. Following the internal procurement process will take more time. A supplier has submitted an offer to provide the needed raw material and reimburse the cost later, however the cost is very high. Which of the following should Frank do? (1) Submit change request with extra budget. (2) Update the procurement strategy. (3) Negotiate with the supplier. (4) Reject the offer and escalate to procurement department. (5) Update the procurement management plan.
explanation: The objective of the procurement strategy is to determine the project delivery method, the type of legally binding agreement(s), and how the procurement will advance through the procurement phases. The project manager should negotiate with the supplier to get less price and update the procurement strategy to allow faster delivery methods. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 476

text: What is the personality trait of a person who has the ability to perceive the information presented by others and to manage them?
explanation: Emotional character has the ability to perceive emotions and the information they present and to manage them; measure of interpersonal skills. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 66

choices.text: Managerial
Political
Emotional
Social

text: You are a project manager for a four phases mechanical project. You finally produced the deliverable of the first phase and have just delivered it to your customer for acceptance. The customer called you to inform you that some features they were expecting are missing. What's the first thing you should do?
explanation: You must first understand what's the problem. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 83

choices.text: Submit a change request to the project management plan to add the missing features.
Inform the customer that the deliverable has been verified in the Control Quality process.
Apologize to the customer and tell your team to start building missing features.
Meet with the customer to understand the missing features.

text: A major software project with five sub-projects is being performed in your organization. Each of the sub-project managers has developed a risk management plan, identified many risks, analyzed them, and planned for risk responses. It is now three months later. What should you not do?
explanation: Risks in the watch list are non-critical risks; they should only be monitored. There is no need to have risk response plans for them. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 423.

choices.text: Make sure that the sub-project managers have risk response plans for all risks in the watch list.
Make sure that the sub-project managers monitor their project work for new and changing risks.
Make sure that the sub-project managers monitor the identified risks and those on the watch list.
Make sure that the sub-project managers monitor risk triggers.

text: Manuel is managing a mega project similar to the project he has just completed. He used most of the processes and artifacts from the previous project. Midway through the project, he notices that some team members are stuck in their work. They are unsure how to proceed or undertake their work. However, he did not face the same issue in the previous project. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: As the project manager used most of the processes and artifacts from the previous project, he needs to tailor them to the needs of the current project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: More tailoring to guidance and training.
Replace those team members with more skilled ones.
Discuss this issue with the functional managers of those team members.
Reduce their work assignment and add this work to the skilled members.

text: Which of the following is a strategy artifact?
explanation: Only project vision statement is a strategy artifacts which is created prior to or at the start of the project. Project vision statement is a concise, high-level description of the project that states the purpose, and inspires the project team to contribute to the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 184

choices.text: Team charter
Release plan
Project vision statement
Risk-adjusted backlog

text: Which of the following is a good metric that can be used to compare two agile teams?
explanation: Return on Investment (ROI) measures the profitability of an investment by calculating the ratio of the benefits received from the investment to the money invested in it. Velocity is team specific. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 61

choices.text: Velocity
Cycle Time
ROI
Work in progress (WIP)

text: Your team has made great achievements during your project, and you want to reward them for their great performance. Which of the following contains the bonus plan for your project?
explanation: The Resources Management plan includes a “Reward and Recognition” section that describes how you’ll reward your team for good performance Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 319 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Resource Breakdown Structure
The resources management plan
The resource histogram
The resource calendars

text: You are managing a large software project. The project team has just calculated the critical path, you received a call from the PMO manager telling you that one of the web developers will be taken from your project to join another more important project, that of course will cause a resource limitation problem. In light of this, which technique should you use to recalculate project schedule?
explanation: Fast tracking is not applicable as resources are limited, resource optimization techniques like resource leveling or resource smoothing should be used to adjust schedule with resource limitations. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 211

choices.text: Critical Path Method (CPM)
Resource optimization techniques
Monte Carlo
Fast Tracking

text: Mark is a project manager for a tele-communications project in his organization. Tom is his sponsor and he is working with David, the risk manager in the organization to determine the effectiveness of the risk responses. Tom and David are concerned that some of the risks assigned to Frederick, a project team member to implement the planned risk responses may not be addresses to the depth they would like. In this scenario, who is responsible for ensuring that this process is performed at an appropriate frequency?
explanation: The project manager is responsible for ensuring that risk audits are performed at an appropriate frequency, as defined in the project’s risk management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 456

choices.text: Mark
David
Tom
Frederick

text: It has been noticed by the agile coach that the team is working extra hours in order to complete the current iteration. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: It is true that the team should maintain a constant pace, that's why they should commit only to what they can deliver according to the user stories sizes estimates and the team velocity. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 9

choices.text: The team should commit to complete less stories in the next iterations.
Extra resources should be added to maintain constant pace.
The team should increase their velocity.
Recalculate the team velocity and move some user stories back to the product backlog.

text: Which of the following risk response strategies indicate that the project management plan will not be changed?
explanation: Risk acceptance is a risk response strategy whereby the project team decides to acknowledge the risk and not taking any action unless the risk occurs. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Exploitation
Transference
Mitigation
Acceptance

text: Ben is the project manager of the new construction project in his organization. He is currently performing the Quantitative Risk Analysis process. He is using a software where the project schedule is computed many times with the input values chosen at random for each iteration to create a probability distribution from the iterations. What technique is Ben using?
explanation: The mentioned technique is the definition of simulation using Monte Carlo analysis. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 433 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 177

choices.text: Bubble Chart
Sensitivity analysis
Decision tree
Monte Carlo analysis

text: Which of the following statements indicates the optimal cost of quality?
explanation: The optimal COQ is one that reflects the appropriate balance for investing in the cost of prevention and appraisal to avoid failure costs. So costs of conformance should be less than costs of nonconformance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 282 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 88

choices.text: Appraisal costs should be less than prevention costs.
Costs of conformance should be less than costs of nonconformance.
Prevention costs should be more than external failure costs.
Internal failure costs should be less than prevention costs.

text: The seventh principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Working software is the primary measure of progress". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: In agile projects, teams assess progress based on the emerging product or service they are creating. Teams want to focus instead on the primary goal of the project which is a product that delivers value to the business. So, Teams are focused on flowing work through the system to completion and not beginning new work until work in progress is completed. They try to remove bottlenecks and have a cap on items in each queue to limit work in progress. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Daily Standups
Work in progress (WIP)
Retrospectives
Timeboxing

text: Which of the following is among the scrum master's roles during the daily scrum?
explanation: The daily scrum is conducted by the team for the team. The scrum master is a coach, he teaches the developers to keep the daily scrum within the 15 minutes timebox. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: Teach the developers to keep the daily scrum within the 15 minutes timebox.
Facilitate the discussion to ensure that each team member has a chance to speak.
Ensure that each developer has answered the three questions.
Lead the discussion and intervene when needed.

text: A project is being implemented using adaptive approach. Midway through the project, a quality issue has been discovered that prevents releasing a key deliverable to the customer. The product owner wants to analyze the root cause of the issue. In light of this, what should the team facilitator do?
explanation: The team facilitator is a servant leader. He needs to guide the team members and help them implement the agreed solution. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 41

choices.text: Coach the team members on how to reach consensus on proposed solutions.
Let the team members decide what to do as they are self organized.
Facilitate a meeting with relevant stakeholders to collect more ideas.
Discuss analyzing the root cause with the team and implement the agreed solution.

text: A risk has remained even after you implement all of your risk response strategies. What kind of risk is this and what should you do about it?
explanation: Residual risks don’t need any further analysis. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: It’s a secondary risk; you should add it to the watchlist and revisit it periodically.
It’s a residual risk; you need to eliminate it by eliminating its root cause.
It’s a residual risk; you need to add a contingency reserve for it.
It’s a top risk, you should try to reduce its probability or impact.

text: Tony and Maxim are two project managers in two different organizations. Tony is usually involved in evaluation and analysis activities prior to project initiation which includes consulting with executive and business unit leaders on ideas for advancing strategic objectives and improving organizational performance. While Maxim is always assigned to manage projects in his organization after the project charter is signed. Which of the following statements best describes this situation?
explanation: The role of a project manager may vary from organization to organization. The project management role is tailored to fit the organization. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 51

choices.text: Tony is more experienced than Maxim, so he is included in evaluation and analysis activities prior to project initiation.
Tony's organization is always looking for advancing their strategic objectives and improving their organizational performance, while Maxim's organization don't.
Both Tony and Maxim perform their roles as project manager each one as specified in his organization.
Tony is performing the role of a portfolio manager, while Maxim is performing his role as a project manager.

text: A project team is working on a mechanical project. It is the fifth project that this team is working on. The team members have developed a synergy due to working together on many projects, the best leadership style to lead this team is to:
explanation: Performing teams are autonomous, empowered, self-managing, and self-policing. They require little more than to be pointed in the right direction and given regular recognition and appreciation for their high performance. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 30

choices.text: Provide a more directive than supportive leadership.
Provide a more supportive than directive leadership.
Provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership.
Provide low levels of both directive and supportive leadership.

text: A project manager wants to incorporate some agile practices into his plan-driven project. He suggests for the team to attend a daily standup. What might be needed from the team prior to attending a daily standup in a plan-driven project?
explanation: The organization's tools might include burndown charts, burnup charts, Kanban boards,..etc.They are powerful information radiators used to reflect the team's progress towards their goals and objectives. Updating individual's progress prior to the daily standup meetings enables the team to collectively see their progress. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 53

choices.text: Try to remove any impediments to individual's work progress.
Update the progress of their tasks on the organization's tools.
Send a progress report to the project manager for the tasks assigned to each individual.
Complete the work mentioned in the previous standup.

text: For which of the following can a work breakdown structure be used?
explanation: WBS doesn't show durations, relationships, or reasons to initiate a project. It is a component of scope baseline which is used as a basis of comparison between actual performance and what was planned. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 161 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 84

choices.text: Showing the relationships between activities
Communicating with the customer
Showing durations for each work package
Showing the reasons to initiate the project

text: All of the following are stakeholders satisfaction measures, except:
explanation: Stakeholder satisfaction can be measured with surveys or by inferring satisfaction, or lack thereof, and by looking at related metrics, such as morale, net promoter score, mood chart and turnover. Throughput analysis assesses the number of items being completed in a fixed time frame. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 103

choices.text: Mood Chart
Net Promoter Score
Throughput analysis
Morale

text: During an audit on Paul's agile project by PMO in his organization, they found that there are iteration plans for the current and next iteration only. The PMO representative asks Paul where are the plans for the remaining iterations of the project. What is the best course of action?
explanation: Progressive elaboration refers to the process of adding more detail as new information emerges. Agile methods rely on progressive elaboration to first create, and then refine, many kinds of project assets to make them increasingly accurate as the team learns more about the project. These “progressively elaborated” assets might include plans, estimates, requirements definition,..etc. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 58

choices.text: Accept a red flag for having incomplete plan and discuss it with the team in next retrospective.
Inform the PMO representative that the project is being progressively elaborated.
Ask the team to create plan for the remainder of the project.
Create detailed plans for the remaining iterations of the project.

text: George is the project manager for a construction project. He is currently reviewing the latest work performance report from his team. The report contains the earned value measurements to this point, forecasts and variances. Which of the following values contribute to the greatest risk in the project?
explanation: TCPI is the ratio between work remaining and budget remaining, if it is greater than one, this means that work remaining is greater than budget remaining, which means high risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 105

choices.text: TCPI = 1.05
Schedule Variance SV = 1,500$

text: During discussing the project status with some stakeholders in the weekly project status meeting, the project manager realizes that he should add two new risks to the risk register, four new requirements to the requirements documentation and requirements traceability matrix, and three new stakeholders to the stakeholder register. Does he need formal approval from the sponsor or others to change these?
explanation: The risk register, requirements documentation, and stakeholder register are all project documents; they are not part of the project management plan. Project documents are created by the project manager for his own needs to help him in managing his project. They don't require a formal change request to update them. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 89 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Yes. All documents require formal approval of changes by management.
It depends on what changes are being made.
Yes. The change needs to go through the Change Control Board for approval.
No. These are part of project documents, not the project management plan.

text: Which of the following approaches can be considered as a hybrid of both agile and waterfall methods?
explanation: Estimating and planning in story points is an agile approach and using traditional vendor contracts based on product specifications is a waterfall approach. Using Gantt charts and WBS are both waterfall approaches. Using pull system, daily standup, co-location and delivering in releases are all agile approaches. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 36

choices.text: Using Gantt charts for tracking progress with a WBS for planning the work.
Holding daily stand-ups while using a pull system to move work through the process.
Estimating and planning in story points while using traditional vendor contracts based on product specifications.
Co-locating the team while delivering the product in releases.

text: Two team members are having a disagreement over order of activities in your project. A team member thinks that you should document everything before starting work, while the other thinks you can do the work while you finish the documentation. You meet with both of them and listen to their argument. Then you decide that you will document most of it first but will start doing the work when you are 80% done with the documentation. What conflict resolution technique are you using?
explanation: Each party has to give up something. This is an example of compromise. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 169

choices.text: Forcing
Confronting
Smoothing
Compromise
